title: gen_id not lexicographically sortable as doc claims
description:
src/id.rs doc comment says 'Lexicographically sortable within a single process' but the format is "{millis}-{seq:06}". The millis field has no fixed width — it's currently 13 digits but will become 14 digits on 2286-11-20. More immediately, there's no guarantee it won't be fewer digits if system time is wrong.

What goes wrong: nothing today (millis is 13 digits for the next 260 years), but the doc claim is technically false for the general case, and a reader trusting the doc might build ORDER BY on these IDs assuming lex sort = time sort, which works only by coincidence of current epoch.

Fix: either pad the millis field to a fixed width (e.g., {millis:016}) to make the claim true, or change the doc comment to say 'sortable within a single process for the current epoch (millis field is unpadded)'. The padding approach is better since it costs nothing.
